warmup.text: 5 min incline treadmill walk (speed 5, incline 4)
warmup.sub: Gets blood into legs and hips

warmup.text: 10 bodyweight squats - slow (3 sec down, 1 sec up)
warmup.sub: Pattern the squat movement

warmup.text: 10 hip circles each leg + 10 glute bridges
warmup.sub: Activates glutes before loading them

warmup.text: 10 leg swings front/back + 10 lateral each leg
warmup.sub: Opens hip flexors and adductors

exercises.name: Barbell Back Squat
exercises.tempo: 3 sec down / 1 sec pause / drive up
exercises.weight: Start at 60% of your max. Add 2.5 kg each week.
exercises.cues: Setup: Bar on upper traps, feet shoulder-width, toes 15-30° out. Descent: Push knees out in line with toes, chest stays tall - don't let it cave forward. Depth: Hip crease below knee if mobility allows, parallel minimum. Drive: Push the floor away, squeeze glutes at the top. Don't lock knees aggressively.

exercises.name: Romanian Deadlift
exercises.weight: Lighter than you think. Feel the stretch, not the load.
exercises.cues: Setup: Barbell or DBs, hip-width stance, soft bend in knees. Hinge: Push hips back as bar slides down your thighs - the bar should drag your legs the whole way. Range: Go until you feel a strong hamstring stretch (mid-shin for most). Drive: Push hips forward to stand - not a back extension. Squeeze glutes hard at top.

exercises.name: Walking Lunges
exercises.weight: Hold dumbbells at sides. Start light - form first.
exercises.cues: Step: Long stride forward, drop back knee toward floor without slamming it. Front knee: Stays stacked over ankle, doesn't cave inward. Torso: Upright - don't lean forward. Drive: Push off front heel to step through. This also trains your football stride pattern.

exercises.name: Leg Press
exercises.weight: Heavy enough to feel it by rep 10. Not so heavy form breaks.
exercises.cues: Foot position: Shoulder-width, mid-platform. Range: Lower until knees near 90° - don't let lower back peel off the pad. Push: Through the whole foot, not just toes. Lock: Don't fully lock knees at top - keeps tension on muscle.

exercises.name: Hanging Leg Raises
exercises.weight: Bodyweight. Can progress to ankle weights later.
exercises.cues: Hang: Dead hang, shoulders packed (not shrugged to ears). Raise: Bring legs up by tilting pelvis - don't just swing hips. Goal: Legs parallel or higher. Lower: Slow controlled descent - this is where most of the work happens. No kipping.

exercises.name: Plank
exercises.weight: Bodyweight. Progress to 60 sec over time.
exercises.cues: Setup: Forearms on floor, elbows under shoulders. Body: Straight line from head to heel - no hips in the air, no sagging. Activation: Squeeze glutes, brace abs as if about to take a punch. Breathe: Don't hold your breath. Steady inhale/exhale throughout.

cooldown.sub: Kneeling lunge against wall. Best hip flexor stretch you can do.
Flat on back, pull one leg toward chest with straight knee.
Or figure-4 stretch if pigeon is too intense. Deep glute release.
Decompress the lower back after squatting.

warmup.text: 2 min slow walk to start treadmill session
warmup.sub: Let heart rate rise gradually

warmup.text: Ankle circles - 10 each direction, each foot
warmup.sub: Prep ankle joints for balance work

exercises.name: Treadmill / Brisk Walk
exercises.weight: Bodyweight. Incline 2-3 to increase effort without raising HR too much.
exercises.cues: Target HR: 130-140 BPM. If you don't have a monitor, use the talk test - you can speak in full sentences but wouldn't want to sing. Treadmill: Speed ~5.5-6.5 km/h at incline 2-3. Outdoor: Brisk walk pace where you're breathing noticeably but not panting. Consistency: Don't let pace surge - keep it steady the entire time.

exercises.name: Single-Leg Romanian Deadlift
exercises.weight: Light DB (5-10 kg) or bodyweight to start. Balance first.
exercises.cues: Stand: On one leg, soft bend in standing knee. Hinge: Push hips back, let torso lower and back leg rise simultaneously - like a seesaw. Spine: Neutral throughout - don't round. Balance cue: Focus on a spot on the floor 1m ahead. Return: Squeeze glute of standing leg to drive back to upright. If wobbling badly, hold a wall to learn the pattern first.

exercises.name: Single-Leg Calf Raises
exercises.tempo: 2 sec up / 1 sec hold / 2 sec down
exercises.weight: Bodyweight on a step edge for full range. Progress to holding a DB.
exercises.cues: Position: Stand on the ball of one foot on a step edge, other foot crossed behind. Lower: Let heel drop below step level for full stretch. Rise: Drive up as high as possible onto toes. Balance: Use fingertips on wall only for balance, not support. This is your ankle injury prevention exercise - critical for football.

exercises.name: Bosu Ball / Balance Stand
exercises.weight: Bodyweight. Progress: eyes open → eyes closed → eyes closed on Bosu.
exercises.cues: If Bosu available: Stand on flat side (dome down) on one leg. If not: Stand on one leg on flat floor - eyes closed to add challenge. Focus: Micro-adjustments through the foot and ankle - this is the work. Arms: Out to sides for balance. Progression: Week 1 eyes open, Week 2 eyes closed, Week 3+ add small arm movements while balancing.

cooldown.sub: Pull ankle to glute, stand tall. Balances the hip flexor work.
Arms in goalpost position, lean through doorframe. Counters forward posture.
Cross ankle over knee, hinge forward gently.
Cat-cow x 10, thoracic rotations x 10/side, world's greatest stretch x 5/side.

warmup.text: 5 min light rowing machine or arm bike
warmup.sub: Warms shoulders and upper back without loading them

warmup.text: Band pull-aparts x 15 or face pulls x 15 (light)
warmup.sub: Activates rear delts and rotator cuff before pressing

warmup.text: Arm circles - 10 forward + 10 backward each arm
warmup.sub: Shoulder mobility prep

warmup.text: Cat-cow x 10 reps
warmup.sub: Thoracic spine warm-up for overhead pressing

exercises.name: Barbell / DB Bench Press
exercises.weight: Start at a weight you can do 10 reps cleanly. Add 2.5 kg/week.
exercises.cues: Setup: Feet flat on floor, slight arch in lower back, shoulder blades pinched and pulled down. Grip: Slightly wider than shoulder width. Descent: Bar to lower chest (nipple line), elbows at 45-75° from torso - not flared out wide. Drive: Push bar up and very slightly back. Common mistake: Letting shoulder blades unpin at bottom - keep them retracted throughout the rep.

exercises.name: Bent Over Barbell Row
exercises.weight: Match your bench press weight to start. Balance pushing and pulling.
exercises.cues: Setup: Hip-width stance, hinge forward to 45°, slight knee bend, back flat. Grip: Just outside shoulder width, overhand (or underhand to hit biceps more). Pull: Drive elbows back and toward hips - not up. Row to lower rib cage. Squeeze: 1 sec hold at top with shoulder blades squeezed. Lower: Fully extend arms - full range matters.

exercises.name: Overhead Press (DB or BB)
exercises.weight: Lighter than bench. Strict press - no bouncing from legs.
exercises.cues: Standing preferred (more core engagement). Start: Bar at shoulder height, elbows slightly forward. Press: Drive bar straight up - slightly behind head at top (bar travels over the head, not in front). Core: Brace hard - don't hyperextend lower back. Seated DB option: Both DBs pressed simultaneously, end position with pinkies slightly higher than thumbs.

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown
exercises.weight: Should be challenging by rep 10. Don't use momentum.
exercises.cues: Grip: Slightly wider than shoulder width, overhand. Lean: Very slight backward lean (10-15°). Pull: Drive elbows down and back - imagine trying to put elbows in back pockets. Pull bar to upper chest. Stretch: Let arms fully extend at top - feel the lat stretch. Common mistake: Pulling with biceps not lats - focus on the elbow driving down.

exercises.name: Incline DB Curl
exercises.weight: Light-moderate. The incline increases stretch - you'll need less weight.
exercises.cues: Setup: Bench at 45-60°, arms hang straight down behind body. Curl: Supinate (rotate) wrist as you curl up - pinky higher than thumb at top. Squeeze: Hard contraction at top for 1 sec. Lower: 3 sec controlled - don't let gravity drop your arms. The stretch at the bottom is the growth stimulus.

exercises.name: Tricep Pushdowns (cable) / Dips
exercises.tempo: Lock out at bottom / 2 sec return
exercises.weight: Cable pushdown: medium weight. Full lockout every rep.
exercises.cues: Pushdown: Elbows pinned to sides - they must not move. Push handle straight down until arms fully locked. Return: Slow and controlled back to start. Dip alternative: Bench dip or parallel bar dip, going deep enough to feel tricep stretch at top. Key: The lockout at the bottom is where triceps fully contract - don't cut it short.

cooldown.sub: Pull elbow across chest. Rear delt and rotator cuff release.
Hand behind head, elbow pointing up. Post-pressing relief.
Elbows at 90°, lean through door. Counters bench press tightening.
Lie on side, knees bent 90°, rotate top arm to open chest to ceiling.

warmup.text: 2 min slow walk / gentle pace to ease in
warmup.sub: No formal warm-up needed before Zone 2 - just start easy

exercises.name: Treadmill / Brisk Walk / Cycle
exercises.weight: Bodyweight. Bike at resistance level 5-7 or treadmill at 5.5-6.5 km/h incline 2.
exercises.cues: Same rules as Tuesday Zone 2. Target 130-140 BPM. You should be able to speak in complete sentences. Variety tip: Alternate between treadmill and bike week-to-week to reduce boredom. No music with a fast BPM - it unconsciously drives you to go harder than Zone 2. Podcasts work better for this session.

exercises.name: Dead Bug
exercises.tempo: Slow and deliberate - 3 sec per rep
exercises.weight: Bodyweight. Can hold a small plate above chest to progress.
exercises.cues: Setup: Lie on back, arms pointing at ceiling, knees bent 90° above hips. Move: Simultaneously lower opposite arm (overhead) and opposite leg (extend to hover above floor). Key: Lower back must stay flat on floor throughout - if it arches, you're going too far. Return: Slowly back to start. Exhale as you extend. This trains the exact core stability needed for football contact situations.

exercises.name: Pallof Press
exercises.weight: Cable or resistance band at chest height. Light enough to hold position steady.
exercises.cues: Setup: Stand side-on to cable/band anchor, hold handle at chest with both hands. Press: Extend arms straight out in front of chest - the cable tries to rotate you, resist it. Hold: 2 sec extended, feel the anti-rotation work. Return: Controlled back to chest. Key: Hips and shoulders stay square throughout. This is exactly the core pattern used when shielding the ball in football.

exercises.name: Side Plank
exercises.weight: Bodyweight. Progress to 50 sec, then add a hip dip for reps.
exercises.cues: Setup: Forearm on floor, elbow under shoulder, feet stacked or staggered. Lift: Raise hips - body in a straight diagonal line from head to feet. Don't: Let hips sag down or pike up. Stack: Hips fully extended, core braced. Breathe: Don't hold breath. Progress: When 35 sec feels easy, add a hip dip - lower hip to tap floor and raise back up.

cooldown.sub: One knee on floor, other foot forward. Gently push hips forward and down. Undoes sitting posture and pre-empts Sunday football.
Seated on floor, cross one leg, rotate toward crossed leg with both hands on knee.
Mobilises the spine after the cardio session.

warmup.text: 5 min bike at easy pace (resistance 4-5)
warmup.sub: Low impact warm-up before heavy hinging

warmup.text: 10 Romanian deadlifts - empty bar or very light
warmup.sub: Groove the hinge pattern before loading it

warmup.text: 10 banded monster walks - 5 steps each direction
warmup.sub: Activates glutes before deadlift. Use a light resistance band above knees.

warmup.text: Hip 90/90 stretch - 60 sec/side
warmup.sub: Sits hips into the position needed for deadlift setup

exercises.name: Conventional Deadlift
exercises.tempo: Controlled setup / drive hard / lower with control
exercises.rest: 2 min between sets - this one earns full rest
exercises.weight: Build from 70% of estimated max. Add 5 kg/week. Form > weight always.
exercises.cues: Setup: Hip-width stance, bar over mid-foot, hinge and grip just outside legs. Brace: Big breath into belly (360° brace), chest up, lats locked (imagine protecting your armpits). Pull: Push the floor away - don't think 'pull up'. Hip hinge: Bar stays dragging up your shins and thighs the entire lift. Lockout: Hips and knees extend simultaneously - squeeze glutes hard. Lower: Hinge back, controlled. Don't drop the bar.

exercises.name: Bulgarian Split Squat
exercises.weight: Bodyweight first session. Add DBs once balance is secure. Hardest exercise in the plan.
exercises.cues: Setup: Rear foot elevated on bench, front foot far enough forward that front shin is vertical at bottom. Descend: Drop straight down - don't lunge forward. Back knee toward floor. Front knee: Tracks over toes, doesn't cave in. Drive: Push through front heel to stand. Common mistake: Front foot too close - causes forward lean and knee stress. If knees hurting, step forward more.

exercises.name: Farmer Carry
exercises.weight: Heavy DBs or plates - aim for challenging. Start at 20 kg/hand.
exercises.cues: Pick up: Deadlift them up - don't round back to grab. Walk: Tall posture, shoulders back and down, core braced. Don't: Let shoulders creep up to ears or let the load pull you sideways. This builds: Grip strength (helps all pulling lifts), upper trap thickness, and spinal stability under load. One of the highest-return exercises for overall strength.

exercises.name: Cable Face Pulls
exercises.weight: Light. This is health work, not a strength exercise.
exercises.cues: Setup: Cable at forehead height, rope attachment, step back. Pull: Elbows up and back - pull rope toward face, hands ending beside ears. External rotation: At the end of the pull, rotate hands so thumbs point back (like a 'double bicep' pose). Squeeze: 1 sec hold at full contraction. Why: Counters the internal rotation from all the pressing this week. Keeps shoulders healthy long-term.

exercises.name: Ab Wheel Rollout
exercises.weight: Bodyweight. Start with partial range if needed (wall stop).
exercises.cues: Start: Kneeling, wheel directly under shoulders, arms straight. Roll out: Let wheel roll forward as you lower body toward floor - spine absolutely neutral (no sagging hips). Range: Start at 50% extension, build to full over weeks. Pull back: Contract abs hard to pull wheel back to start. Key: This is NOT a hip flexor exercise - the abs must be the anchor. If lower back hurts, reduce range immediately.

cooldown.sub: Best post-deadlift hip release. Hold in the deep stretch.

cooldown.text: Child's pose with lateral reach - 45 sec each side
cooldown.sub: Reach arms to each side in child's pose to open the lat.
Kneeling lunge, push hip forward and slightly rotate toward front leg.

cooldown.text: Foam roll lower back and glutes - 2 min
cooldown.sub: Sit on roller, cross one ankle over knee, roll glute side to side. Don't roll the spine directly.

warmup.text: No structured warm-up needed - the walk IS the warm-up
warmup.sub: Start slow and build pace over first 5 min

exercises.name: Outdoor Walk / Light Jog
exercises.short: Easy Zone 2 - saving legs for Sunday
exercises.weight: Bodyweight. No treadmill today if possible - outdoor walk preferred.
exercises.cues: Purpose today: Flush the legs from the week's training, get blood moving, without digging into the recovery you need for Sunday football. Pace: Notably easier than Tuesday/Thursday Zone 2. If HR is above 130, slow down. Outdoor benefit: Uneven terrain activates ankle stabilisers passively - good pre-football stimulus. Target: 6,000-8,000 steps minimum.

exercises.name: Full Body Stretch Routine
exercises.tempo: Static holds - breathe into each stretch
exercises.rest: Move directly from one to next
exercises.weight: Bodyweight.
exercises.cues: In order: 1) Kneeling hip flexor stretch (60 sec/side) - most important for football. 2) Standing quad stretch (45 sec/side). 3) Seated hamstring stretch (45 sec/side). 4) Lying glute/piriformis stretch - figure 4 (45 sec/side). 5) Doorframe chest opener (60 sec). 6) Child's pose (60 sec). Do this in order. Don't rush it - this is your Sunday prep.

exercises.name: Dead Hang
exercises.weight: Bodyweight on any pull-up bar.
exercises.cues: Let go: Fully decompress - shoulders should rise toward ears, entire spine lengthens. Don't: Engage lats or try to hold a tight position. This is passive. Benefit: After a week of loading, hanging passively reverses the compressive forces on the spine. Also: Grip endurance improves weekly - helps your Thursday and Friday lifts.

cooldown.text: Calf stretches - 60 sec/leg against a wall
cooldown.sub: Straight leg (gastrocnemius) then bent knee (soleus). Football destroys calves - prep them now.

cooldown.text: Ankle circles + foot rolling on tennis ball - 2 min
cooldown.sub: Roll sole of foot on a tennis ball. Activates intrinsic foot muscles for Sunday.

warmup.text: 5-8 min light jog around the pitch
warmup.sub: Don't start drills cold. Blood needs to be moving first.

warmup.text: Dynamic leg swings - 10 front/back + 10 lateral, each leg
warmup.sub: Opens hips actively - better than static stretching pre-match

warmup.text: High knees + butt kicks - 2 x 20m each
warmup.sub: Activates hip flexors and glutes for sprinting

warmup.text: Lateral shuffles - 2 x 10m each direction
warmup.sub: Wakes up the agility patterns you'll use in the match

warmup.text: 3-4 short explosive accelerations (50% effort)
warmup.sub: Primes the nervous system for match-speed movement

exercises.name: Drill Session
exercises.weight: N/A
exercises.cues: Treat drills seriously: Every direction change is balance training. Every passing drill is coordination work. Focus areas: First touch, receiving under pressure, quick release, positioning. Your gym work pays off here: The Bulgarian split squats = better deceleration. The core work = better balance under contact. Don't hold back - this is an active training session, not a social warmup.

exercises.name: Match Play
exercises.weight: N/A
exercises.cues: This is your HIIT session for the week. Heart rate will spike to Zone 4-5 (165-185 BPM) during sprints and defensive pressures. Positioning: Use your improving fitness to work hard off the ball - this is where matches are won. Hydration: Drink water at every break - at 105 kg you sweat significantly. Half-time: Light jog / walk - don't sit down completely or legs will stiffen.

cooldown.text: 5 min slow walk after final whistle - don't stop suddenly
cooldown.sub: Lets heart rate come down gradually. Prevents blood pooling in legs.
Pull ankle to glute. Quads take huge load in football.
Most critical post-football stretch. Sprinting tightens hip flexors severely.
Legs straight, reach toward feet. Hold without bouncing.

cooldown.text: Standing calf stretch against wall - 45 sec/leg
cooldown.sub: Calves cramp overnight if you skip this. Straight leg first, then bent knee.
On your back, cross ankle over knee, pull both legs toward chest.
Lie on side, rotate top arm to open chest. Undoes the hunching from fatigue.
